review-details_isCollapsed__Bsg_9: Berger Paints delivered an exceptional experience during my home renovation project. Their customer service was commendable, offering valuable guidance in choosing colors and finishes. The paint quality was outstanding, with vibrant colors, excellent coverage, and quick drying. I appreciated their eco-friendly approach, using low VOC paints for a healthier indoor environment. The durability and stain resistance of their paints impressed me, maintaining a fresh appearance even after months. I highly recommend Berger Paints for their commitment to customer satisfaction, eco-friendly products, and overall high-quality results, making them an excellent choice for any painting project.
review-details_isCollapsed__Bsg_9 2: Berger Paints has some drawbacks to consider, including higher prices, a limited color range, and potential issues with paint coverage and drying time. Additionally, availability may be limited in certain locations, and a few customers have reported unsatisfactory experiences with customer service. While the brand receives positive reviews overall, potential buyers should weigh these cons against its strengths when making a paint selection.

review-details_detailsLink__NxIv5: Work at Berger Paints India Ltd
review-details_isCollapsed__Bsg_9:
1. Second largest paint manufacturer in India and one among the most valued brands.
2. Good scope for learning on the job.

review-details_isCollapsed__Bsg_9 2:
1. Highly political environment.
2. Poor clarity regarding job description, responsibilities and  accountability leading to discrimination and undue favoritism by local regional heads & managers.
3. Poor facilities at most of the depots- some depots do not have even clean & decent toilet facilities.
4. Very poor work- life balance. You are called to work on weekly off days & public holidays except national holidays( 15th Aug etc).
5. Career progression in management cadre for long time employees is very poor(long process of tests, interviews & several intermittent grades for a same position) as compared to recruits from competitors who are similarly qualified and experienced.
6. Too much focus on hierarchy & poor official access to top management.
7. Weak and incompetent HR department.
8. Very poor salary & benefits as compared to other paint manufacturers especially in the field staff & junior management cadre. Here again direct recruits from competitors or other companies are paid significantly higher than those employees who have come up through the ranks. 
9. Local admin staff come under the purview of Finance & Accounts & harass the field staff for even getting their travel and other reimbursements- some cashiers even demanding a cut for giving timely payments.

review-details_detailsLink__NxIv5: A cup of Politics and unqualified managers
review-details_isCollapsed__Bsg_9: Sales experience, salary on time
review-details_isCollapsed__Bsg_9 2:
1. ZERO work-life balance. Examples:
- You are expected to remain available 24x7, which means you will get calls at 5 pm on Sunday or at 11:30 pm on a weekday and you are expected to answer and do what you are asked. If you dont, your appraisal will be dinged.
- If someone calls you and you are unable to answer for 10-15 minutes for whatever, reason, that is enough time for someone to 'escalate' your 'unavailability' to your manager.
- By policy they 'allocate' you 30 leaves for the year. In reality you will be lucky to get 6. That too has to be earned though continuous overwork throughout the year.
- If you leave office before 7:30, you will eventually be scolded for leaving early very frequently.
- All saturdays are working. Not just that, you will be expected to work on a lot of sundays. You will be specifically assigned work on Saturday evening which is to be submitted Monday morning, to ensure that you work on sunday.

2. Toxic and abusive culture. Examples:
- Verbal abuse, bad language, expletives thrown about casually (and loudly) are very commonplace. HR is fully aware and powerless to change anything.
- Some of the seasoned bosses even make personal insults and vague threats.
- Get ready for either bad language or sarcasm for the smallest mistake.
- Most bosses / middle managers are either unwilling and unable to help you through tough situations.
- Getting work done from other departments is next to impossible and there are frequent arguments and blame games played over email. Expecting a reasonable amount of co-operation from co-workers is a bad idea.

3. Culture of overworking as a norm:
- You are always expected to be stretching. Your appraisal goals are set in such a way that you cannot achieve them by only working 9-10 hours a day for 6 days a week. You HAVE to stretch to achieve even your minimum passing metrics.
- If your superiors find out you are managing your given workload without too much stress or overworking, you will be rewarded with more work in the next quarter.
- If you are not visibly showing stress on your face, the default assumption becomes you do not have enough workload.

4. NO positive feedback whatsoever.....unless you resign and they realise it would be more difficult to train a replacement.
